Descriere
În medicină, ca și în multe alte zone ale economiei, va exista o competiție deosebită pentru noi experiențe de servicii care să iasă în evidență nu doar prin cost redus și productivitate (funcționalitate și standardizare) ci și prin satisfacerea nevoilor individuale, confort și protecție socială (expresie și personalizare). Programul propune o coerență conceptuală și strategică între proiectarea arhitecturilor hardware și software care asigură și managementul optimizat al acestui sistem.
Relevanță pentru piața muncii
Noul program de master aprofundează domeniul informatizării structurilor complexe furnizoare de servicii, răspunzând cerințelor actuale de asigurare de competențe extinse pentru personalul angajat direct sau realizând servicii de consultanță, evaluare, C-D, proiectare, organizare, planificare, logistică și mentenanță pentru servicii medicale. Astfel, programul răspunde unei cereri formulate la nivel mondial în ceea ce privește inovarea serviciilor.
Cunoștințe necesare
Cunoștințe minimale privind modelarea, proiectarea, implementarea și exploatarea sistemelor informatice; Cunoștințe teoretice de bază privind prelucrarea semnalelor.
Competențe și abilități dobândite
- Competențe generale printr-o pregătire aprofundată în domeniul tehnologiilor informatice de conducere a proceselor și structurilor discrete complexe, cu dezvoltare sistematică de aplicații software destinate optimizării sistemului de sănătate din România.
- Competențe specifice rezultate din aprofundarea domeniilor conducerii distribuite a proceselor şi unităţilor medicale şi de management al resurselor acestor unități (inclusiv aplicând concepte și metode ale Inteligenței Artificiale), necesar specialiștilor cu competențe de coordonare și conducere, integratori de procese și arhitecturi informaționale și manageri
Structura cursuri
Anul I, semestrul I
Anul I, semestrul II
Anul II, semestrul I
Anul II, semestrul II
Teme Cercetare
- Ontologii si baze de date relationale pentru medicina translationala
- Concepte si metode stiintifice in modelarea proceselor biologice de crestere
- Sisteme cyber-fizice pentru telemedicina
- Aplicatii ale analizei fractale in medicina si biologie
- Arhitecturi hardware si software pentru sisteme informatice orientate pe servicii in sanatate
- Utilizarea Tehnologiei Informatiei si Comunicatiilor pentru managementul sistemelor de sanatate
Limbaje de programare și tehnologii folosite
C++, Java, Python, Verilog/VHDL
Alte informații
Limba de predare: Română
Companii partenere: Institutul Clinic Fundeni, pentru activitatea de cercetare

Innovative Processes and Products for Improving Quality of LifeAutonomous cognitive systems
The PR403 Laboratory focuses on implementing processes based on the latest discoveries in information technology to develop applications and products that contribute to enhancing the quality of life. It serves as a hub for the research activities of its members, supporting contracted projects, doctoral theses supervised by the laboratory team, and research topics from Master’s programs such as Complex Signal Processing in Multimedia Applications and Information Systems in Medicine.
The main research areas currently pursued by the laboratory team (with potential for further development and expansion) include:
- Hybrid Autonomous Multi-Agent Air-Ground Systems for environmental monitoring to reduce pollution and forecast weather using artificial intelligence, particularly machine learning and neural networks.
- Intelligent Data Management Systems (including images) for monitoring patients at home or in hospitals.
- Medical Diagnostic Support Systems involving data fusion from neural network ensembles.
- Agriculture Monitoring Systems to detect diseases and harmful insects in organic and precision farming using drones combined with AI techniques.
- Automated Inspection and Defect Detection Systems across various domains such as industry, agriculture, and food production.
The laboratory aims to achieve horizontal objectives, including equal opportunities, sustainable development, and innovation. Most of the research activities listed above involve forming interdisciplinary teams or partnerships (e.g., information technology, systems engineering, medicine, agriculture, environment, aviation), as evidenced by ongoing projects, publications, and doctoral research led by the core research team. The laboratory’s focus includes leading or partnering in national and international project competitions, participating in projects directly assigned by interested economic units, publishing in ISI Web of Science Q1, Q2 journals, or prestigious international conferences, and attracting highly qualified young specialists to the team.
The advanced research activities are organized under the guidance of Prof. Dan Popescu and Prof. Loretta Ichim.
Research Services Offered:
- Biomedical Diagnostic Systems using advanced image processing techniques and convolutional neural networks for tumor diagnosis, employing AI-based neural networks for detection, diagnosis, and prediction in various medical specialties.
- Home Monitoring Systems for recently discharged patients or individuals with disabilities.
- Deep Neural Network Systems for multimodal analysis of human emotions through decision fusion.
- Detection and Tracking of Mobile Objects from unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) using modern AI techniques. Detection, segmentation, and evaluation of objects of interest in aerial images using machine learning and neural networks.
- Person and Vehicle Detection and Tracking in restricted areas.
- Intelligent Weather Prediction Models based on data from high-altitude UAVs.
- Smart Systems for Crop Monitoring and detecting dangerous insects using hybrid UAV-WSN (wireless sensor networks) systems.
- Integration of IoT Devices, Machine Learning, and Digital Twin in modern production processes. Designing and testing flexible manufacturing or inspection lines based on AI techniques.
Completed Research Projects:
- HALYomorpha halys Identification: Innovative ICT tools for monitoring and sustainable management of the brown marmorated stink bug and other pests. Dan Popescu – Project Director, ERA NET COFUND, CT-AGRI-FOOD, 2019 Joint Call, registered at UEFISCDI PN III 202/2020. 2020-2024.
- Integrated System for Dynamic Loading of High-Voltage Power Lines based on online monitoring of operational and environmental parameters. Dan Popescu – Project Manager, contract PTE 38/2020, UEFISCDI Subprogram 2.1 – Competitiveness through research, development, and innovation. 2020-2022.
- Intelligent and Distributed Control of 3 Complex Autonomous Systems integrated into emerging technologies for medical-social personal assistance and servicing of precision flexible manufacturing lines. Radu Dobrescu – Project Manager, UEFISCDI, 78_PCCDI/2018, Complex Projects, 2018-2021.
- Integrated UAV-WSN-IoT Intelligent Monitoring System for Precision Agriculture (MUWI). Dan Popescu – Project Director, 1224/22.01.2018, 2018-2020.
- Integrated Multi-Agent Aerial Robotic System for exploring terrestrial regions of interest (MAARS). Dan Popescu – Project Director, Research, Development, Innovation Program for Space Technology and Advanced Research – STAR, Romanian Space Agency (ROSA), contract 185/2017, 2017-2019.
- Multi-Drone System for Evaluating Flood Effects (SIMUL). Dan Popescu – Project Director, BRIDGE GRANT – Knowledge Transfer to Economic Agent, UEFISCDI 49BG/2016, 2016-2018.
- FPGA-Based Multifunctional Module for image processing in industrial robotics applications. Loretta Ichim – Project Manager, PN III: Program 2 – Increasing the Competitiveness of the Romanian Economy through Research, Development, and Innovation, Innovation Vouchers, NR. 62CI/2017, Beneficiary ASTI CONTROL SA.
- Research, Innovation, and Development Ecosystem of TIC products and services for a society connected to the Internet of Things (NETIO), Dan Popescu – Technical Lead for Research Themes, Operational Competitiveness Program 2014-2020.

În atenția masteranzilor UPB din anul I
Înscrieri la Programul de formare psihopedagogică în vederea certificării competenţelor pentru profesia didactică, Nivelul II
Departamentul de Formare pentru Cariera Didactică și Științe Socio-Umane (DFCD-SSU) din Universitatea POLITEHNICA din București (UPB) organizează, în anul universitar 2020-2021, Programul de formare psihopedagogică Nivelul II pentru studenții programelor de masterat din UPB, care au absolvit Nivelul I al programului în cadrul studiilor de licență. Programul de formare psihopedagogică, Nivel II, se desfășoară pe parcursul celor 4 semestre ale programului de masterat la care sunteți înscriși.
Ce conține dosarul de înscriere?
a) copie diplomă de licenţă / adeverinţă care să demonstreze finalizarea ciclului de licenţă, eliberată de secretariatul facultăţii absolvite;
b) copie certificat de absolvire Nivel I al programul de formare psihopedagogică (cu durata de trei ani, derulat în perioada facultăţii)/ adeverinţă care să demonstreze absolvirea Nivelului I, eliberată de secretariatul facultăţii absolvite;
c) fișă de înscriere la Nivelul II;
d) declarație pe propria răspundere care confirmă absolvirea programului de studii universitare de licență;
e) declarație pe propria răspundere care confirmă absolvirea programului de formare psihopedagogică Nivelul I;
f) chestionar online, în care evidențiați motivația, aşteptările şi compatibilitatea dvs. cu specificul competenţelor pentru profesia didactică;
g) acord de prelucrare a datelor cu caracter personal.
Cum se realizează înscrierea?
Înscrierea pentru Nivelul II se realizează online, în perioada 2 octombrie- 15 octombrie 2020, prin completarea formularului de înscriere (c-g):
https://www.surveygizmo.eu/s3/90276667/chestionarmotivational2
Pentru a încărca documentele care atestă absolvirea programului de studii universitare de licență și a programului de formare psihopedagogică Nivelul I (a-b)*, accesați link-ul:
https://www.surveygizmo.eu/s3/90277850/Validare-inscriere-nivel-II
* Prin excepție, candidații care au promovat examenul de licență în UPB în sesiunile corespunzătoare anului universitar 2019-2020 nu trebuie să trimită adeverințele care atestă absolvirea programului de studii universitare de licență și a programului de formare psihopedagogică Nivelul I (a-b).
* Candidații absolvenți ai altor universități, care nu pot obţine în timp util aceste documente (a-b) de la secretariatele facultăţilor, își vor completa dosarul pentru Nivelul II în termen de două luni de la data admiterii. Linkul va fi activ până la 10 decembrie 2020.
Cum se organizează concursul de admitere?
Concursul de dosare are loc în data de 16.10.2020. Rezultatele (pe bază de calificativ: admis/ respins) și orarul pentru semestrul I al anului universitar 2020-2021 vor fi afişate la avizierul DFCD-SSU și pe site-ul Departamentului, în secțiunea Anunțuri (www.dppd.upb.ro) în data de 19 octombrie 2020.
Pentru informații suplimentare
E-mail: dfcdssu@upb.ro
Website: http://dppd.upb.ro/Program-de-formare-psihopedagogica-pe-parcursul-studiilor-universitare-de-masterat/

Innovative Products and Processes for Knowledge Extraction
The PRECIS 305 Laboratory is dedicated to research and educational activities focused on knowledge extraction from data (data mining), structured data storage (data warehousing), and data processing. This laboratory supports research activities for its members and for graduate students under their guidance, especially in research disciplines and related academic courses such as ABD (Advanced Database Design) and MTI (Multimedia Technology and Internet). The lab is also utilized for doctoral research conducted by students under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Eng. Alexandru Boicea and Lect. Dr. Eng. Ciprian Octavian Truică.
Research Services Offered:
- Data Collection Support:
- Assistance in collecting data from multiple data sources.
- Data Preprocessing Support:
- Data cleaning
- Data integration
- Data transformation
- Data reduction
- Data discretization
- Data Mining Services:
- Frequent itemset mining in transaction datasets
- Regression analysis
- Classification (decision trees, rule-based classifiers, Bayesian classifiers, support vector machines, kNN, ensemble methods like Bagging, Boosting, Random Forest)
- Semi-supervised learning (using both labeled and unlabeled data for training)
- Clustering (centroid-based, distribution-based, density-based, hierarchical, fuzzy)
- Web mining applications (content, structure, usage mining)
- Data Storage and Utilization Applications:
- Support in structuring data warehouses and using them effectively.
- Development of applications using Oracle and Microsoft data storage and processing technologies.
- Design and application support for NoSQL systems: MongoDB, Cassandra, RIAK, and platforms like Hadoop, Spark, HBase.
Completed and Ongoing Research Projects:
International Collaborations:
- COST Action CA19102: Language In The Human-Machine Era (LITHME)
- COST Action CA19130: Fintech and Artificial Intelligence in Finance – Towards a Transparent Financial Industry (FinAI)
- COST Action CA18131: Statistical and Machine Learning Techniques in Human Microbiome Studies (ML4Microbiome)
- COST Action CA18209: European Network for Web-centred Linguistic Data Science (NexusLinguarum)
- COST Action CA18231: Multi-task, Multilingual, Multi-modal Language Generation (Multi3Gen)
- COST Action CA17137: A Network for Gravitational Waves, Geophysics, and Machine Learning (G2Net)
- COST Action IC1406: High-Performance Modelling and Simulation for Big Data Applications (cHiPSet)
- NETIO Project: Research, Innovation, and Development Ecosystem for ICT Products and Services in a Society Connected to the Internet of Things, POC Project, Contract No. 53/05.09.2016
- ERRIC Project: Empowering Romanian Research on Intelligent Information Technologies, FP7-REGPOT-2010-1/264207
- Erasmus-Mundus Master Programme: Data Mining & Knowledge Management, Contract No. 159648-EM-1-2009-1-FR-ERA MUNDUS-EMMC, 2009-2014
Hardware and Software Resources:
- Hardware:
- 16 Lenovo All-in-One stations with Windows OS
- 6 HP All-in-One stations with Linux Ubuntu OS
- Video projection system
- Video conferencing system (telepresence)
- Two servers dedicated to the laboratory
- White magnetic board
- Software:
- Databases: Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL
- Apache Hadoop cluster for data processing using MapReduce and Apache Spark
- NoSQL document-oriented database cluster: MongoDB
- NoSQL column-oriented database cluster: Apache Cassandra
- NoSQL key-value database cluster: RIAK
- Data warehouse cluster: HBase
Research Team Members:
- Prof. Dr. Eng. Florin Rădulescu – Co-responsible
- Prof. Dr. Eng. Alexandru Boicea – Co-responsible, PhD supervisor within the Doctoral School of Automatic Control and Computers
- Lect. Dr. Eng. Ciprian Octavian Truică – PhD supervisor within the Doctoral School of Automatic Control and Computers
- Lect. Dr. Eng. Dan Schrager
- Lect. Dr. Eng. Sorin Ciolofan
- Asst. PhD Candidate Eng. Daniel Popeanga
- Asst. PhD Candidate Eng. Izabela Alexandra Oprea

Vedere Artificială
Înțelegerea modului de funcționare a unui sistem de vedere artificială. Cunoașterea modalităților de reprezentare a imaginilor numerice. Însușirea cerințelor privind achiziția și memorarea imaginilor. Formarea și conversia analog-numerică a imaginilor alb-negru și color. Însușirea modului de funcționare a unei camere de luat vederi și de cuplare a acesteia cu sistemele de calcul. Deprinderea metodelor și tehnicilor specifice de prelucrare a imaginilor (prelucrare primară, de nivel mediu și de nivel înalt) în scopul îmbunătățirii reprezentării și în scopul extragerii informației. Evidențierea unor structuri hardware și software de prelucrare în timp real a imaginilor. Analiză și interpretarea imaginilor complexe: texturi și fractali. Prezentarea unor aplicații tipice ale vederii artificiale: roboți industriali, imagistică medicală, control automat, coduri de bare. La finalul cursului și al laboratorului, studenții vor fi capabili să achiziționeze și să prelucreze imagini în scopul îmbunătățirii reprezentării și în scopul segmentării și clasificării obiectelor din imagini.

În perioada 15 – 19 Iunie 2015 se vor depune la secretariat formularele completate împreună cu 2 fotografii color 30/40mm și o copie după cartea de identitate.

Artificial Intelligence for SEnsing, Low-Power Networking and ICS SEcurity (AISENSE)
The goal of the AISENSE Laboratory, within the PRECIS Research Institute, is to develop new concepts, methods and experimental prototypes that integrate state-of-the-art ubiquitous sensing, communication and security modalities in intelligent automation applications for industry. Three main research and engineering pillars of our activity thus relate to:
- Intelligent measurement systems that enhance core sensing functions with on board data processing, learning and inference capabilities for real-time analysis of collected multi-dimensional data streams;
- Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) architectures for embedded distributed sensing, computing and low-power scalable communication and their interoperability within open automation architectures for efficiency and productivity;
- Industrial Control System (ICS) Cybersecurity at the interface of technical means for intrusion detection, prevention and anomaly detection, and standardisation and regulatory approaches for increasing OT robustness and seamless IT/OT integration, with extensive domain expertise.
We carry out interdisciplinary research at the interface of automation and industrial informatics with domain verticals in manufacturing, energy, utilities and logistics, with a focus social, economic and environmental sustainability and resilience of artificial intelligence systems.
The AISENSE Laboratory offers a range of research, innovation and engineering services and welcomes new partnerships with private or public sector organisations in the following domains:
- Industrial data management of measurement and engineering data through data quality assessment, domain-specific pre-processing and feature extraction;
- Data pipeline development and data-driven modelling of industry and energy processes using novel machine learning algorithms and time series methods;
- Design, testing and evaluation of distributed sensor systems with low-power mesh protocols for scalable data collection;
- Interoperable and open technologies for cost-effective and productive automation;
- Pre-auditing of ICS cybersecurity solutions integrating SoA methods, algorithms and tools for risk management and regulatory compliance;
- Personnel training for cyber-awareness, cyber-hygiene and basic cyber training of essential operators of complex, networked, automation solutions in accordance to international best practices (NIST, CISA, ENISA).
Members of the laboratory coordinate and participate in multiple research projects with local (UNSTPB), national (UEFISCDI) and international (Horizon Europe, Digital Europe, Horizon Cascade Funding) through a network of collaborations with complementary research groups and innovative companies.

href_text: Premiul Henri Coanda pentru Stiinte Aplicate al Marii Loji Nationale din Romania, in colaborare cu Academia Romana, pentru profesorii Dumitru Popescu si Dan Ştefănoiu
content:
Premiul Henri Coanda pentru Stiinte Aplicate al Marii Loji Nationale din Romania, in colaborare cu Academia Romana, pentru profesorii Dumitru Popescu si Dan Ştefănoiu
29.06.2015
Pe data de 21 iunie 2015 s-au decernat premiile Marii Loji Nationale din Romania, în colaborare cu Academia Româna, in cadul unui spectacol de gala la Ateneul Roman.
Premiul Henri Coanda pentru Stiinte Aplicate a fost obtinut de un grup de 4 autori: prof. Dumitru Popescu si prof. Dan Ştefănoiu din facultatea noastra, acad. Florin Gh. Filip (Academia Romana) si prof. Pierre Borne (Lille, Franta) pentru lucrarea “Optimizari in stiintele ingineresti”. Cartea a fost publicata in limbile franceza si engleza la prestigioasele edituri Hermes-Lavoisier (Paris), respectiv John Wiley and Sons (Londra). Pentru anuntarea acestui premiu, laudatio a fost sustinut de catre acad. Ioan Dumitrache – presedintele sectiei de Stiinte Aplicate.
Spectacolul de gala a fost transmis pe TV2 si TVR HD si poate fi vizionat la adresele de mai jos. Premiul Henri Coanda a fost anuntat in prima parte, incepind cu minutul 30 al inregistrarii.
Prima parte:
http://www.tvrplus.ro/editie- gala-premiilor-marii-loji- nationale-din-romania-346789

Concursul FameLab
Au început înscrierile pentru selecțiile regionale ale competiției FameLab, ediția 2015 Famelab International își caută câștigătorii! A început cursa pentru înscrierile în cadrul celei de-a opta ediții a competiției internaționale care urmărește să descopere tinere talente în comunicarea științei! Studenti si cadre didactice cu varsta între 18 și 35 de ani care studiaza sau predau la Universitatea Politehnica Bucuresti sunt asteptati cu o prezentare de 3 minute din domeniile științei, tehnologiei, matematicii sau ingineriei la selecția regională care va avea loc în ziua de 7 aprilie în cadrul festivalului POLIFEST. Câștigătorul finalei naționale va reprezenta România la finala FameLab International care se va desfășura în cadrul Festivalulului de Știință de la Cheltenham în luna iunie.
Toate detaliile pe site-ul British Council:
http://www.britishcouncil.ro/programe/educatie/famelab si in curand pe site-ul POLIFEST
Asteptam reprezentati de la toate facultatile din UPB!
Persoana contact: Elena Pop
Tel: +40 (0)75 206 4124
E-mail: elena.pop@upb.ro

Description
The “Service Engineering and Management” (SEM) master program responds to a worldwide demand for the growth and innovation of services. As services become an increasingly important part of value creation in modern societies and economies, their complexity and scope increase, becoming globally dispersed. Thus, it becomes necessary to model, develop and operate integrated systems for client-centred, sustainable services, based on interdisciplinary approaches (engineering, management, marketing, human psychology) with IT support using advanced technologies: Web, Cloud, SOA.
Relevance to the job market
The courses taken in the SEM master’s program offer the following range of knowledge:
- Modelling and implementation of business process flows in the field of services;
- Development of IT applications for services using modern technologies: SOA, web, cloud;
- Capacity planning for services; adapting capacity to demand; automating front- and back-office processes.
Required knowledge
Computer programming, databases, communication protocols, information systems and computer networks, data flow modelling, discrete event systems.
Competences and skills acquired
- Working with scientific concepts and methods in interdisciplinary fields;
- Integration of concepts and methods specific to economic sciences and marketing in the development of services;
- Integration of the principles of psychology and communication in the development of services;
- Modelling and implementation of business process flows in the field of services;
- Development of SOA (Service Oriented Architecture), ESB (Enterprise Service Bus) and SaaS (Software as a Service) architectures;
- Development of IT applications for services;
- Web, Cloud and automation technologies for services
Course structure
Year I, semester I
Year I, semester II
Year II, semester I
Year II, semester II
Research Themes
- Performance evaluation for classification and clustering algorithms
- Modelling systems for services
- Software system for the management of IaaS Cloud services
- System for checking and enforcing security policies in a Cloud system
- Capacity planning in public transport services
- Optimizing the allocation of resources for job shop and flow shop processes
- Orientation of manufacturing and automatic driving processes towards services
- Aggregation of microservices in the Bluemix environment
- Services with IoT technologies
- Cloud models and services for GPU-based infrastructures
- Web services with mobile devices
- Robotic Process Automation (RPA) of back-office processes for services
Programming languages and technologies used
JAVA/JADE, ILOG CPLEX OPL, Excel (Solver, Data analysis), C++, SQL, Linux, BASH, Matlab, Cloud Bluemix, MySQL, DB2, BluePrism
Other information
Teaching language: English
Partner companies: IBM, ORTEC
International partnerships: ERASMUS

ANUNT CONCURS BURSE SEE
07.11.2014
ANUNT Burse SEE

Caravana ARIA la UPB
Caravana ARIA la UPB
Universitatea POLITEHNICA din Bucureşti va fi gazda caravanei ARIA în data de 10 noiembrie 2014, începând cu ora 10.00, în sala ED109 din incinta Facultăţii de Automatică şi Calculatoare.
Caravana ARIA este un proiect coordonat de către Asociația Română pentru Inteligență Artificială (ARIA), alături de partenerii săi academici: Universitatea din Craiova, Universitatea “Aurel Vlaicu” din Arad, Universitatea “Politehnică” din București, Universitatea “Alexandru Ioan Cuza” din Iași, Universitatea “Ovidius” din Constanța, Universitatea Tehnică “Gheorghe Asachi” din Iași, Universitatea “Babeș-Bolyai” din Cluj-Napoca, Universitatea de Vest din Timișoara, Universitatea Tehnică din Cluj-Napoca și vizează regruparea și animarea comunității științifice în inteligență artificială (IA) din România. Universitatea POLITEHNICA din Bucureşti este membru fondator al ARIA.
Caravana este de asemenea susținută și de mediul economic prin Discovery Networks Europa Centrală & de Est, divizie a Discovery Communications (NASDAQ: DISCA, DISCB, DISCK), lider mondial pe segmentul canalelor TV cu plată, care ajunge la peste 2,5 miliarde de abonați în peste 220 de țări și teritorii.
Caravana ARIA se adresează profesorilor, cercetătorilor și specialiștilor în IA, studenților cu profil tehnic și tinerilor pasionați de probleme complexe în general, precum și oamenilor de business și liderilor de opinie interesați de cercetare și inovare tehnologică.
Caravana ARIA va poposi, în total, în 9 orașe unde va organiza întâlniri cu actorii principali în IA din orașul respectiv și va face o prezentare a asociației pentru studenții cu profil tehnic și tinerii pasionați de IA.
Caravana ARIA își propune mai multe obiective, ce includ: realizarea unei analize detaliate a comunității științifice în IA din România; crearea de noi canale de comunicare între actorii principali în IA din România; promovarea ARIA, a misiunii și obiectivelor acesteia, regruparea comunității științifice în IA și implicarea activă în caravană a principalelor centre de cercetare în IA din România.

REZULTATE CONCURS BURSE SEE
25.11.2014
rezultate_selectie_burse_SEE

Obiective curs
Prezentarea domeniului inteligentei artificiale si a principalelor subdomenii cu accent pe sistemele bazate pe cunostinte. Structura generala a unui sistem de inteligenta artificiala : sistem cognitiv, sistem rezolutiv. Modalitati de reprezentarea cunostintelor, consistenta bazelor de cunostinte. Sisteme rezolutive, cautarea solutiei in adancime, largime, cautare optimala, algoritm A, A*. Rationarea in conditii de incertitudine: abordarea Bayes, coeficient de certitudine, multimi vagi. Limbaje de programare logica, clips, fuzzyclips.
Obiective laborator
Dobandirea abilitatilor de programare logica in Prolog. Strategii de rationamente Prolog, backtracking. Sisteme bazate pe regului de productie: Exsys, Clips, Fuzzyclips. Implementarea listelor in Prolog, definirea si utilizarea predicatelor, implementarea structurilor de control.
Conținut curs
- Definitii, clasificari, scurt istoric inteligenta artificiala.
- Structura generala si componentele sistemelor bazate pe cunostinte.
- Limbaje formale, gramatici, similitudine, inferenta, lant inferential.
- Reprezentarea cunostintelor: clauze, limbaje de ordinul intai, frame, retele de productie, retele semantice.
- Sisteme rezolutive, faze sistem, tipuri de rationamente.
- Rationarea prin control inainte: cautare in adancime, cautare in largime, cautare exhaustiva, algoritm A, algoritm A*.
- Rezolvarea problemelor prin decompozitie : cautarea in arbori grafuri SI/SAU.
- Rationare cu incertitudini : bazata pies probabilitate Bayes, utilizarea coeficientului de certitudine, sisteme fuzzy.
- Sisteme bazate pe cunostinte pentru aplicatii in timp real.
- Studii de caz.
Conținut laborator
- Limbajul Exsys Convid pentru reprezentarea cunostintelor prin retele de productie.
- Limbajul Clips cu extensia Fuzzyclips.
- Dezvoltare de aplicatii in Exsys si Clips.
- Programare logica, limbaj Prolog.
- Organizarea clauzelor in Prolog.
- Utilizarea listelor in Prolog.
- Dezvoltarea de aplicatii Prolog.

Colocvii de evaluare a practicii
Colocviile de practica pentru studentii de la Calculatoare se vor desfasura in zilele de 29 si 30 septembrie intre orele 9-14. Sala va fi anuntata cu o saptamana inainte.
La colocviul de practica se vor aduce urmatoarele acte:
-
atestatul de practica
-
caietul de practica
Studentii care au probleme sa realizeze numarul de credite necesare pentru trecerea anului fara practica sunt rugati sa aduca actele pe 17 septembrie (9-14 ef308) sau alternativ sa le trimita scanate prin e-mail la vlad.posea@cs.pub.ro
In cazul in care nu puteti ajunge pe 29-30 septembrie la colocviu mai puteti aduce actele in urmatoarele date:
-
17 septembrie 9-14 Ef308
-
23 si 25 septembrie 9-14 Ef308
Pentru studentii din cadrul proiectului Impuls colocviul se va desfasura tot in zilele de 29-30 septembrie. Documentele aduse insa sunt specifice proiectului, va rugam consultati site-ul proiectimpuls.ro
Mai multe informatii aici: http://wiki.cs.pub.ro/studenti/practica/practica-2013-2014#colocvii-de-evaluare-a-practicii

Calculatoare și Tehnologia Informației (CTI) pentru sesiunea septembrie

Update: Tabere studenteşti
Update:
Termenul de depunere al cererilor de tabere studentesti (gratuite), finantate din fondurile MTS, se prelungeste pana vineri 30 mai 2014 inclusiv.
Va asteptam la secretariatul facultatii pentru depunerea acestora.
In perioada 21-27 mai 2014 se primesc la secretariatul facultatii cereri pentru taberele studentesti de vara (tabere gratuite).
Modelul de cerere este urmatorul [fisierul atasat] sau se poate obtine si de la secretariat sau de pe situl www.mts.ro.
Metodologia detaliata este afisata la avizierul de la decanat (este disponibila in extenso si pe www.mts.ro).
Pot primi bilet de tabara studentii la licenta sau master avand pana in 35 de ani, finantati de la bugetul de stat, integralisti in anul universitar anterior (!).
Numarul de locuri si locatiile vor fi comunicate ulterior (25-27 iunie 2014), conform metodologiei.
Repartizarea pe perioade si locatii se face in ordinea punctajului obtinut (in principal dupa medie), in perioada 30 iunie-2 iulie 2014, dupa un program care se va comunica ulterior.
Primirea repartizarii se face personal.

Campionatul de Fotbal Robotic
17.03.2014
Facultatea de Automatica si Calculatoare, impreuna cu Inventeaza.ro si EESTEC organizeaza intre 19 martie si 23 mai 2014 Campionatul de Fotbal Robotic. Acesta este un concurs de inteligenta artificiala si robotica ce combina invatatul practic pe platforma Arduino cu distractia jocului de fotbal robotic sub indicatiile regulamentului FIRA ce vizeaza competitiile de fotbal robotic pentru clasa mijlocie la nivel mondial. Inscrierile pentru competitie se fac la adresa www.fotbalrobotic.ro
Evenimentul de lansare a proiectului se va derula, miercuri, 19 martie 2014, incepand cu orele 12:30, in Sala Senatului din cadrul UPB, Splaiul Independentei, nr. 313, sector 6.

href_text: Posibilitati de redistribuire a studentilor pe disciplinele optionale la programele de masterat CTI
content:
S-a afişat repartizarea studenţilor la disciplinele opţionale pe programele de masterat CTI: http://acs.pub.ro/optiuni_master_2013
Repartizarea s-a făcut în limita locurilor disponibile la fiecare curs, pe baza optiunilor fiecarui student. Studenţii care nu au avut loc la nici una dintre disciplinele optate, au fost repartizaţi automat la discipline din grupul G.
Cei care nu sunt mulţumiţi de repartizare pot face cerere de mutare la disciplinele cu locuri rămase disponibile (vezi tabelul de mai jos).
De asemenea, cei din anul 2 de masterat care au fost repartizaţi la o disciplină din grupul G pe care deja au studiat-o (o au deja in contractul de studii) trebuie să îşi aleagă altă disciplina dintre cele cu locuri disponibile (vezi tabelul de mai jos).

Plata restantelor
11.10.2013
UPDATE: Plata restanţelor se efectuează pentru toţi anii de studiu până pe data de 29 octombrie.
Pentru studenţii care au plătit taxele şcolare pentru anul universitar 2013-2014 până la data de 30 septembrie 2013, nivelul taxelor este cel practicat pentru anul universitar anterior(2012-2013).
Începând cu data de 1 octombrie 2013, taxele de şcolarizare au nivelul prevăzut în HS/26.09.2013. În particular, începând cu 1 octombrie 2013, taxele de refacere a disciplinelor sunt:
- TD1 – pentru studenţii care au maxim 2 discipline de refăcut 30lei/cts
- TD2 – pentru studenţii care au mai mult de 2 discipline de refăcut – 50lei/cts
- TD3 – pentru studenţii care nu promovează anul de studii – 65lei/cst

Candidații integraliști pentru sesiunea septembrie 2013 vor depune lucrarile de licența si le vor incărca pe site-ul studenti.pub.ro, in fata unui membru al comisiei, pana pe data de 12.09.2013.
Candidații care mai au restante, ce vor fi susținute pană pe data de 15.09.2013, inclusiv, vor depune si vor incarca lucrările, in fata unui membru al comisiei, pe site-ul upload.cs.pub.ro (Calculatoare), acs.curs.pub.ro (AIS+AII) pană pe data de 12.09.2013. In data de 15.09.2013, după promovarea tuturor restantelor si primirea dreptului, acestea vor fi incarcate si pe site-ul studenti.pub.ro.

Training 3D-UPB
13.03.2013

Aerial Image Understanding & High Performance Scientific Computing
The High Performance Scientific Computing Group of the Computer Science Department from the Faculty for Automatic Control and Computers provides scientific researchers from academia, industry and research, significant computing resources to solve scientific and engineering problems studied by these groups. The laboratory provides information about the availability and usage of existing computing systems or software libraries, and projects in progress as well as practical advice designed to facilitate the use of these systems within the DataCenters of the UNSTPB.
We are a team of PhD students and experienced researchers with an ambition to push the frontiers of AI and take important steps towards making the technologies of tomorrow possible. We aim to design and implement efficient image processing algorithms, that could be used onboard UAVs and UGVs. Our main objectives are:
- To create fast methods for online and unsupervised learning in large spatiotemporal volumes of data, that have the capabilities of functioning in dynamic, real-world scenarios. We intend to use different kinds of imaging and 4D (3D + time) sensing capabilities, ranging from fixed sensors to cameras present on UAVs. Given the huge amounts of unlabeled data available and the costly manual annotation, unsupervised learning is crucial for the development of new AI technologies. Therefore, we will focus on efficient learning methods without human supervision.
- To develop methods capable of complete scene understanding, from the level of objects and activities involving objects to translating the visual scene into natural language.
- To give drones the capacity to “see” and understand the world in which they fly.

Alexandru SoriciAssistant Professor, PhD

Ambient Intelligence

education
- PHD IN COMPUTER SCIENCE AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY: Thesis co-supervised between the Faculty of Automation and Computers, University Politehnica of Bucharest and Ecole Nationale Superieure des Mines, Saint-Etienne, France.
Dr. Alexandru Sorici is an Assistant Professor at UPB and a member of the AI-MAS research laboratory. Alexandru holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from UPB and École Nationale Supérieure des Mines de Saint-Étienne (a joint thesis defended in 2015). The thesis aims to create a middleware that allows the design and programming of context-aware applications. The emphasis of the thesis is on expressiveness in modelling and reasoning, as well as on increased flexibility in configuration and implementation. The proposed system incorporates a lot of techniques from the fields of semantic web and multi-agent systems.
His research interests encompass context management, agent-oriented programming, human activity recognition, and ambient intelligence (which extends to research in assisted robotics, autonomous driving, and wearable device data analysis in a medical context). Alexandru has published over 45 research papers in international journals and conference proceedings.
Recent research activities and projects include working on context-management in Hypermedia-based Multi-Agent Systems (director of project CASHMERE), studying the predictive power of data from wearable devices and patient logs in determining the health status of patients suffering from neurological diseases (HORIZON 2020 ALAMEDA project), and working on techniques of adaptive AI applied to time series analysis and medical data as part of the AI Lighthouse project ENFIELD.
Alexandru also serves as the Secretary of the Romanian Association for Artificial Intelligence (ARIA). In this role, he has contributed to organizing several events aimed at education and raising public awareness about Artificial Intelligence, including TiCIA (Young Researchers in AI – 2015, 2016, 2018), EEML 2019, Romanian AI Days, and the AI4Highschool initiative.

Bogdan-Costel MocanuProfessor Habil.
areas of expertise
- Distributed Systems
- P2P systems
- BigData systems
- Scheduling
- Information security
education
Postdoc, Doctoral School of Automatic Control and Computers, National University of Science and Technology POLITEHNICA Bucharest, Romania
Bogdan-Costel MOCANU is an Assistant Professor at the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, Department of Computer and Information Technology of the National University of Science and Technology POLITEHNICA Bucharest. He earned his Ph.D. in 2019 and continuously demonstrated remarkable growth and improvement in his expertise and contributions in multiple research areas, including Peer-to-Peer systems, Distributed systems, Cloud computing, Big Data, Real-time scheduling, and Information security.
If knowledge can create problems, it is not through ignorance that we can solve them. – Isaac Asimov
Bogdan-Costel MOCANU is an Assistant Professor at the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, Department of Computer and Information Technology of the National University of Science and Technology POLITEHNICA Bucharest. He earned his Ph.D. in 2019 and continuously demonstrated remarkable growth and improvement in his expertise and contributions in multiple research areas, including Peer-to-Peer systems, Distributed systems, Cloud computing, Big Data, Real-time scheduling, and Information security. His robust academic background, spanning nearly a decade of intensive research and publication, includes a prolific output of 10 ISI-indexed journal articles—two classified in Q1 and four in Q2—and 22 conference papers, complemented by a book chapter indexed in Web of Science. Reflecting his scholarly impact, his work has garnered 129 citations on Google Scholar, including 51 citations on Web of Science, achieving an H-index of 5 (Web of Science), H-Scopus index of 4, and H-Scholar of 7.
His research journey began during his master’s studies in Advanced Software Systems, under the mentorship of Professor Laura Gheorge, where he researches information security, and developed the dissertation thesis entitled “Framework for Security Evaluation of the Performance of Cryptographic Algorithms”. His early academic output included two papers on this subject. With a growing interest in Distributed Systems, Cloud computing, and Peer-to-Peer networks, he started collaborating with professors Florin Pop, Ciprian Dobre, and Valentin Cristea. Following his master’s program, he pursued a PhD. in 2014, researching distributed Peer-to-Peer networks under the supervision of professors Valentin Cristea and Florin Pop. His doctoral thesis, “Trusted Auto-Adaptive Peer-to-Peer Overlays,” defended in 2019, was awarded with Magna Cum Laude distinction, resulting in eight publications: 2 ISI-indexed journal articles, 1 book chapter, and 5 conference papers.
Following his PhD, Bogdan Mocanu maintained a close colaboration with his mentors, Professors Florin Pop and Ciprian Dobre, which led to his career shift from government to academia as Assistant Professor at UPB. In 2021, he also took a Postdoctoral Researcher role within the Doctoral School of Automatic Control and Computers, where he continued exploring new applications of distributed systems, and optimization in real-time systems. His postdoctoral research contributed to projects like “ODIN112 – Real-time Speech-to-Text Transcription System for Romanian Language and Emotional State Recognition in 112 Emergency Calls”, which featured an innovative IVR solution that improved emergency response systems in Romania. His postdoctoral thesis, “Multicriteria Scheduling with Real-Time Constraints,” reflects his enduring commitment to this field. He also collaborated on the “CREAM: Collision Risk Estimation and Automatic Collision Mitigation between Satellites” project, where he contributed to the research and developed of a platform for satellite coordination benefiting the European Space Agency.
In his academic career, Bogdan Mocanu has contributed significantly to both European and national research initiatives. His project leadership is exemplified by his role as project director for two national and one international research grants: (i) “EduCEC – Modern distributed platform for educational applications in Cloud-Edge-Continuum”. This project focuses on a the research and development of a modern distributed educational platform supporting data science education through a Cloud-Edge-Continuum platform with JupyterHub integration over Kubernetes; (ii) “NextEDR – Next generation agent-based EDR systems for cybersecurity threats”. His role in this project was to design and implement a MVP of cybersecurity threat mitigation platform suing a Cloud-Continuum model and (iii) The European “Digital4Sustainability – Accelerating the Digital & Green Transition in the ICT Industry” project that involves the development of courses for VET and SME on digital sustainability.
Additionally, he has actively contributed to multiple international and national projects and research grants. The most significant contributions are: (i) Design and development of an international Master Program in Cybersecurity Management in “Digital4Security – European Masters Programme in Cybersecurity Management & Data Sovereignty” project; (ii) Member in the “NetZeroCities – National Competence Centre and solutions for the development of Climate Neutral and Smart Cities” project and (iii) Lecturing at the summer schools of “LLSF – Education 4.0: Living Labs for the Students of the Future” project, in Bucharest (2023) and Lisbon (2024) on topics related to data science and big data.
In his teaching role, Bogdan Mocanu has taught various courses and applications, including Introduction to Big Data (course – master 1st year), Distributed Systems (project – master 1st year), Distributed Concurrent Systems (application – bachelor 4th year), Programming languages and programming (application – bachelor 1st year), Numerical Methods (application – bachelor 1st year), Network protocols (application – bachelor 2ndyear) and Advanced Software Engineering (course – bachelor 4th year). Also, since 2023 he is Associated Professor at the Military Technical Academy Ferdinand I, where I teach Parallel and Distributed Algorithms (bachelor 4th year). For the Advanced Software Engineering course, in 2022, Bogdan Mocanu have won a prize awarded by ANIS Romania in collaboration with Adobe Romania for the Big Data section. Throughout his academic career, he has supervised undergraduate and master’s projects and contributed to PhD. coordination teams, establishing himself as a dedicated teacher and researcher in computer science and engineering.
Top publications

- Mocanu, B. C., Filip, I. D., Ungureanu, R. D., Negru, C., Dascalu, M., Toma, S. A., … & Pop, F. (2022). Odin ivr-interactive solution for emergency calls handling. Applied Sciences, 12(21), 10844.
- Mocanu, B. C., Stoleriu, R., Mocanu, A. E., Negru, C., Drăgotoiu, E. G., Moisescu, M. A., & Pop, F. (2024, March). NextEDR-Next generation agent-based EDR systems for cybersecurity threats. In 2024 32nd Euromicro International Conference on Parallel, Distributed and Network-Based Processing (PDP)(pp. 183-190). IEEE.

PhD in Systems Engineering
Vasile Calofir has over 12 years of interdisciplinary expertise in systems theory, electric, pneumatic, and hydraulic actuations, as well as the modeling and simulation of nonlinear processes. His research visibility is supported by the publication of 3 books, 2 book chapters, and 43 specialized articles indexed in international databases.
„Se știe că un profesor bun e cel care te face ca lucrurile mai grele să ți se pară ușoare.”
— Grigore Moisil
Vasile Calofir is an Associate Professor at the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, within the National University of Science and Technology POLITEHNICA Bucharest. Throughout his academic career, he has been involved in teaching courses on Systems Theory, Electric Machines and Actuations, and Intelligent Actuation Systems. In February 2024, he earned his PhD in Systems Engineering with a thesis titled “Monitoring and Control Systems for Nonlinear Processes.”
His research activity includes participation in 6 institutional development projects and 9 research projects, notably including “Digital Platform for Estimating Seismic Building Degradation Using Machine Learning Techniques (DIGITERRA)” and “e-Learning Solutions in Energy (SeLE),” where he served as project director.

Habil. PhD. Eng. Costin-Gabriel Chiru (624 citations, h-index 14) is an Associate Professor at the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers from University Politehnica of Bucharest. In the past, he worked as a Teaching Assistant (2004-2012) and Lecturer (2012-2017) at the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers from University Politehnica of Bucharest and as a researcher in the field of Natural Language Processing at Human Language Technology Research Institute, Dallas, TX, USA (2005-2006). His PhD Thesis, entitled “The Influence of Repetitions on Discourse and Semantic Analysis”, has been defended in 2011 and was focused on using Natural Language Processing for the analysis of the discourse structure in online conversations.
If you’re going to do something, do it right!
His research in the field was disseminated through over 84 papers published in national and international conference proceedings and journals, including top-tier conferences (CSCL, ITS, ICONIP, ICTAI,) and journals (Computers in Human Behavior, Creativity Research Journal, Applied Intelligence, Applied Sciences, Water, Mathematics). Over the time, Mr. Chiru participated in several national and international R&I projects (FP7 LTfLL, FP7 ERRIC, Erasmus+ QAinAL, CNCSIS K-Teams, CNCSIS Palirom, PNII Cyberwater, PNIII Text2NeuralQL, PN III PED Watergame, Gerontomics, Holotrain). Currently, Costin is interested in projects involving Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing and Time Series Analysis.

Associate Professor Florin Daniel ANTON obtained his MSc. degree in Automatic Control in 2003, and the Ph.D. degree in “Automatic Control” in 2008 of the University Politehnica of Bucharest, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers. Socrates scholarship at the “Université de Valenciennes et du Hainaut-Cambrésis (UVHC)”, ENSIAME faculty (2003). Since 2003 he works at the University Politehnica of Bucharest, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers where he teaches courses and laboratory classes for: Robot Control Systems, Artificial Intelligence in Robotics and Equipments and Systems for Industrial Robot Control. Scientific Director of four national research grants, and team member in over 20 scientific projects in national and international R&D programs. Author and co author of over 70 scientific papers in the field of robotics, artificial vision, distributed information systems, high availability and fault-tolerant systems, disaster recovery published in national and international monographs, journals and conference proceedings. System engineer and Linux trainer in the IBM Linux Competency Centre in Bucharest, he received the RedHat Certified System Engineer diploma in 2008.
“A partnership, by definition, serves both partners, without domination or unfair advantage.” (John F. Kennedy)

areas of expertise

- PhD: Joint Doctorate between University Politehnica of Bucharest (UPB) and Université de Valenciennes et du Hainaut-Cambrésis, 2011
Dr. Silviu Răileanu is an Associate Professor at the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, University Politehnica of Bucharest (UPB). He holds a PhD in Systems Engineering, awarded through a joint program between UPB and Université de Valenciennes et du Hainaut-Cambrésis. His academic contributions span teaching and research, particularly in the areas of distributed control systems, multi-agent systems, and IoT technologies. Dr. Răileanu is responsible for teaching undergraduate courses on Manufacturing Systems Control and master’s courses in Multi-Agent Systems for Enterprise Control, Supply Chain Management, and Logistics. His research focuses on distributed and heterarchical control of discrete event systems, resource planning and allocation in flexible manufacturing systems, robotics, and artificial vision.
“Innovation is the key to solving the challenges of modern manufacturing.”
Dr. Silviu Răileanu earned his engineering degree in Systems Engineering and Computers from the University Politehnica of Bucharest (UPB) in 2005. In 2007, he completed his master’s degree in Open Architecture Systems from the same university. His academic journey was enriched by a Socrates scholarship at Université de Valenciennes et Hainaut-Cambrésis in 2005. In 2011, Dr. Răileanu completed his doctoral studies, which were conducted under a joint supervision agreement between UPB and Université de Valenciennes et du Hainaut-Cambrésis, focusing on distributed and heterarchical control systems.
Currently, Dr. Răileanu is deeply involved in both teaching and research at UPB’s Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers. His teaching portfolio includes undergraduate and master’s courses, where he emphasizes the importance of practical applications in the areas of multi-agent systems and manufacturing systems control. His research contributions include significant work in the optimization of manufacturing processes, robotics, and the integration of IoT technologies into industrial systems. He has participated in several research projects and has a solid publication record in high-impact journals, contributing to the advancement of industrial robotics and distributed control systems.

Ciprian Mihai DobreProfessor Habil.
areas of expertise
- Communication Protocols
- Parallel and Distributed Algorithms
- Web Programming
- Cloud Computing
- Dependable Systems
education
- PhD in Computer Science, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, University Politehnica of Bucharest
Mr. Ciprian Dobre, Vice-Rector of the National University of Science and Technology POLITEHNICA Bucharest, is an accomplished academic and research leader with extensive contributions to the fields of computer science, mobile computing, and digital innovation. Holding the title of Professor Habil. in Computer Science within the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, Ciprian also plays a pivotal role in managing research and quality assurance at the university, where he serves as a member of the Faculty Council. He teaches courses on “Communication Protocols,” “Parallel and Distributed Algorithms,” “Web Programming,” “Cloud Computing,” and “Dependable Systems,” shaping the next generation of computer science and engineering students. His work is deeply rooted in exploring the intersection of Data Science, Mobile and Ubiquitous Computing, the Internet of Things (IoT), Wireless Networks, and Smart Urban Technologies.
As the Coordinator of the Pervasive Services and Products Laboratory (MobyLab) at POLITEHNICA Bucharest, Ciprian drives cutting-edge research in mobile computing, large-scale distributed systems, and pervasive services. He is also responsible for the university’s CRESCDI office, which focuses on the Digitalisation of Research, Development, and Innovation (RDI) activities, ensuring that the institution stays at the forefront of digital transformation in academia and research.
Throughout his career, Ciprian has earned widespread recognition for his scientific contributions. He is a fellow at the California Institute of Technology, a position he has held for over a decade, and has been honored with prestigious awards, including the Oracle PhD Fellowship, the IBM Faculty Award, two CENIC Awards for setting global records in high-speed networking, and four Best Paper Awards for his research publications. His research spans crucial topics such as modeling and simulation, smart technologies, and high-performance computing, contributing to both theoretical advancements and practical applications.
Ciprian’s leadership extends beyond academia. He is a key representative of POLITEHNICA Bucharest in prominent European initiatives such as the European Open Science Cloud (EOSC) and CESAER, an influential forum for universities of science and technology across Europe. His involvement in these organizations positions him at the heart of Europe’s open science and research collaboration movements.
As a Scientific Evaluator for the European Commission and national funding bodies in countries including the Netherlands, Italy, Latvia, and Poland, Ciprian plays a crucial role in shaping the future of research and innovation funding across Europe. His deep understanding of the scientific landscape, coupled with his extensive experience, makes him a sought-after expert for evaluating cutting-edge research projects and national research strategies. He is also a member of the European University Association (EUA) Research and Innovation Strategy Group (RISG), contributing to the development of research and innovation strategies for universities across Europe. In this capacity, he provides strategic advice to the EUA Board on horizon scanning and policy review.
His commitment to advancing research and innovation extends to his collaboration with government bodies. Ciprian has worked closely with the Ministry of Research, Innovation, and Digitalisation, where he developed digital tools for Romania’s National Research Strategy. He has also served as Cabinet Directorand IT Officer within the ministry, further demonstrating his leadership and expertise in the intersection of public policy and digital innovation.
Beyond his academic and governmental roles, Ciprian is also a Senior Researcher 1st Degree with the National Institute for Research and Development in Informatics (ICI Bucharest), where he previously headed the department of Research and Development of Systems and Applications for Society. His research at ICI Bucharest has focused on pervasive and mobile technologies, large-scale distributed systems, and the optimization of e-Government systems, contributing to advancements in both academia and industry.
In addition to his academic and research leadership, Ciprian is the manager of SmartRDI, a Romanian SME that acts as a hub for research and innovation, with a particular focus on mobile computing and IoT technologies. Through SmartRDI, Ciprian fosters innovation in Romania’s tech ecosystem, promoting the development of new technologies that bridge the gap between academia, industry, and government.
In summary, Ciprian Dobre’s career is a testament to his dedication to advancing research, innovation, and education in computer science. His extensive contributions as a professor, researcher, policy advisor, and leader in both academia and government highlight his commitment to shaping the future of technology and science in Romania and across Europe.

Eugen POP received a PhD in Telecommunications in 1999 from University Politehnica of Bucharest, Romania. The thesis focused on applying adaptive algorithms for multiuser signal detection within Code Division Multiple Access systems, CDMA.
He is currently a Head of Works and Senior Scientific Researcher at the National University of Science and Technology Politehnica of Bucharest, Romania, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers. His main research interests areas include e-services development and providing through 5G networks, mobile and wireless communications networks, discrete event dynamic systems, service oriented architectures, cyber physical systems, internet of things, smart cities, intelligent systems, GPS/GIS, automatic vehicle location. He leaded several research and development projects in these domains. He is one of the founding members and representatives of the Romanian working group for the European Technology Platform for communications networks and services NetWorld2020 ETP, former European Technology Platform for Mobile and Wireless Communication – e-Mobility. He is author or co-author of more than 30 research papers presented and published in proceedings of international conferences and journals. He presented scientific papers at conferences organized by the Wireless World Research Forum – WWRF, International Association for Development of the Informational Society – IADIS, at Imperial College of London, etc. He is author of two books and book chapters about digital communications, CDMA systems and e-business services. He has a significant consulting and assessment activity for R&D projects, funded through EU and Romanian financial resources.

education
Eng.D.
Dan-Matei Popovici, is an expert in systems verification with focus on system safety, accuracy, and operational performance. His research includes cloud service policy compliance, MPTCP congestion control, network dataplane verification, and control-plane verification in P4 switches. He is currently working on Large Language Model applications, particularly in educational settings and for generating safe code. He has contributed to five international projects over a decade, holding positions at Clausthal Technical University, ICUB, and CORNET. Currently, he leads UNTSPB-funded and Horizon-Europe FUTURAL projects on LLM research, program verification, and metasearch platforms, collaborating with PhD and postdoctoral researchers, and overseeing student research.
Dan-Matei Popovici specializes in systems verification, focusing on ensuring system safety, accuracy, and operational performance. With over six years of experience, he has developed and refined methodologies and tools for verifying network correctness. His research includes:
- Ensuring safe processing and operator policy compliance in cloud service deployment by network providers.
- Conducting a game-theoretic analysis of MPTCP’s congestion control.
- Supporting network dataplane verification with symbolic execution.
- Verifying control-plane functionality in P4 switches.
Additionally, Dan-Matei Popovici explores LLM performance in educational settings and is currently researching data augmentation and accessibility using Large Language Models.
Dan-Matei Popovici has been a key contributor to five international projects over more than a decade, working with diverse research teams across Europe. His academic roles include positions with the Computational Intelligence Group at Clausthal Technical University, a research fellowship at ICUB (Research Institute of the University of Bucharest), and membership in the CORNET project funded by the European Research Council (ERC).
Currently, Dan-Matei Popovici leads a UNTSPB-funded project aimed at advancing educational methodologies through the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) and program verification to enhance programming education. He is also heading a PN IV (2022-2027) funded project focused on boosting UNTSPB’s capabilities in LLM research and application development. Since June 2013, he has been leading the Horizon-Europe FUTURAL project, where his team is developing an LLM-powered metasearch platform to integrate domain-specific data from smart services across six fields, facilitating easier access to project information.
In these projects, Dan-Matei Popovici collaborates closely with PhD students and postdoctoral researchers, while also overseeing the research activities of Master’s and Diploma students.

I am an Associate Professor of Computer Science at the Computer Science and Engineering Department, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, University POLITEHNICA of Bucharest.
In 2022, I also held a temporary teaching and research position at the Division of Computing Science, Department of Information Technology, Uppsala University, Sweden.
I received my PhD in Computer Science and Information Technology at the Computer Science and Engineering Department, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, University Politehnica of Bucharest, on the topic of Efficient Management of Resource-Intensive Services in Large Scale Distributed Systems. I also hold an M.Sc. Degree in Operating Systems and Distributed Processing and a B.Eng. in Computer Science from the University Politehnica of Bucharest. During my last year of bachelor’s and first year of master’s studies, I was an Intern and then a Junior Research Engineer at the Next Generation Network Infrastructures (NGNI) competence center, Fraunhofer FOKUS Institute, Berlin, Germany, where I worked on computer networking and telecommunication field with a focus on mobile and service orientated architectures.
During my PhD studies, I was an invited researcher at Inria Rennes Bretagne – Atlantique Research Centre, France, in the KerData team, where I worked on Data Management.
I was a postdoctoral researcher at the Microsoft Research Inria Joint Centre, Paris, France, where I worked on Big Data Analytics and Scientific Workflows for Multi-Site Clouds, with a use case in Bioinformatics.
“The best way to predict the future is to invent it.” (Alan Kay)
Elena-Simona Apostol has scholarly and scientific contributions in fields related to Distributed Systems and Machine Learning and is a senior member of the Distributed Systems Research team and the MobyLab laboratory.
Dr. Apostol received her Ph.D. in Computer Science and Information Technology at the Computer Science and Engineering Department, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, University Politehnica of Bucharest in November 2014. For her Ph.D. thesis, she researched different methods to optimize the way virtual resources and tasks are scheduled and managed in Clouds. She also designed a dynamic and flexible service composition engine for services in multi-cloud environments, and she provided an efficient implementation of an efficient searching and indexing model for very large sets of data.
Besides a Ph.D. in Computer Science and Information Technology, she also holds an M.Sc. Degree in Computer Science from the University Politehnica of Bucharest in 2010, and a B.Eng. in Computer Science from the University Politehnica of Bucharest in 2008. During her senior bachelor year and her master’s program, she was an intern and junior research engineer at the Fraunhofer FOKUS Institute, Berlin, Germany, where she worked on Computer Networking and Telecommunications, with a focus on mobile and service-orientated architectures. Dr. Apostol was an invited researcher at Inria Rennes-Bretagne Altlantique Research Centre, France, in 2011 and 2012.
From 2013 to 2014, she has also been a Postdoctoral Research at Microsoft Research Inria Joint Centre, France. During her postdoc, she worked on Big Data Analytics and Scientific Workflows for Multi-Site Clouds, with a use case in Bioinformatics. She was involved in the “Z-CloudFlow- Data WorkFlows in the Cloud” project that dealt with data-intensive geographically distributed workflows on Azure clouds. For this project, she has undertaken research on how to efficiently handle metadata across data centers and how to manage data and tasks so that they can be efficiently placed and accessed during execution.
In 2021, she served as a visiting researcher at the Fraunhofer Institute for Open Communication Systems (Fraunhofer FOKUS), where her research focused on the detection of misinformation and hate speech. Subsequently, in 2022, she held a temporary teaching and research position at the Division of Computing Science, Department of Information Technology, Uppsala University, Sweden.
In 2024, she obtained her habilitation in computer science with a thesis entitled ‘Intelligent Big Data Techniques for Textual Data Management and Analysis’.
Her research topics include Distributed Systems, Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Big Data Analytics, Text Mining, Data Mining, Time Series Analysis, and Cloud Computing. Her work in these domains was published in prestigious conferences (rang A*/A: e.g., PODC- ACM Symposium on Principles of Distributed Computing, ICCS-International Conference on Computational Science, EDBT-International Conference on Extending Database Technology), COLING-International Conference on Computational Linguistics) and top-level journals (e.g., Big Data Research, Neural Computing and Applications, IEEE Access, Information Systems Frontiers, Semantic Web Journal, Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research). Her publication record includes more than 80 peer-reviewed papers: 27+ journal articles, 55+ conference articles, 2 books, and 5 book chapters. Her works have been cited 783+ times according to Google Scholar. Furthermore, her current Hirsch factor is 16 and her i10-index is 28, according to Google Scholar.
She has received several awards for research results in prestigious top Journals as part of the PRECISI UEFSCDI program. She is a member of the organizing or scientific committees of several international conferences and workshops, and she is a scientific referee for several high-impact journals and conferences.
Dr. Apostol has an extensive collaboration network with researchers and experts from all around Europe. As part of this network, she co-written several project proposals, organized workshops, gave talks, and co-authored many joint publications. She has been invited as a researcher at many prestigious universities and research institutes (for example, Aarhus University, Fraunhofer FOKUS, INRIA, and IHE Delft Institute for Water Education), and she worked in several renowned academic research organizations (for example, Uppsala University, Fraunhofer FOKUS, and Microsoft Research).
Dr. Apostol has more than nine years of management experience obtained from national and international projects. She is/was Principal Investigator in three national projects (SCAN-NEWS, StopXocial, ANDESITE), Principal Investigator in a DAAD project on hate speech and misinformation detection, Work Package co-leader in an H2020 project (Data4Water), and an active Management Committee member and Responsible partner for the Romanian team in an EU COST project (RECODIS).
As part of several European research groups, she has been a member of more than 18 national and international research projects.

Cristian FluturPhD
areas of expertise
- Linear Systems
- Signal Processing
- Automatic Control
- Machine Learning
- Descriptor Systems
education
- Ph.D, POLITEHNICA University Bucharest
- MSc, Advanced Automatics
- BSc, Automatic Control and Computer Science
Cristian Flutur conducts his research at the Department of Automatic Control and Systems Engineering, Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers. He obtained his Ph.D. in Systems Engineering in 2018 with a thesis titled “Factorization Techniques for the Regulation of Generalized Systems.” His doctoral research focused on descriptor-type systems with singularities, described by improper or polynomial transfer matrices. He has achieved original results in the fundamental research of singular systems, with key contributions including the simultaneous squaring down of zeros and singular structure, minimal spectral factorization, and Wiener-Hopf type factorization. Recently, Cristian Flutur introduced the Machine Learning Techniques course in the Systems Engineering undergraduate program, where he serves as the course coordinator.
AI should be human-centered, considering the interdisciplinary aspects, oriented to empowering human specific features (creativity, intuition, understanding, consciousness), considering humanities (philosophy, psychology, ethics, aesthetics, and art), and integrating symbolic and connectionist (deep neural networks) paradigms.
Dr. Cristian Flutur is the head of the Dynamic Systems Laboratory in the Department of Automatic Control and Systems Engineering, where he has coordinated various student practice activities. These have resulted in research reports, numerical algorithm software packages, and interactive Matlab graphical interfaces. Some of these activities have extended into bachelor’s and master’s theses with practical applications in sound recognition and quadcopter control.
Cristian Flutur has been an associate professor in the Department of Automatic Control and Systems Engineering since 2018. As of June 2024, he serves as the Vice Dean of the Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers, responsible for international relations.
His main research achievements are as follows:
Simultaneous Squaring Down and Zero Cancellation:
Cristian Flutur investigated and solved the problem of simultaneous squaring down of zeros and singular structure for generalized systems. He parameterized classes of all static and dynamic compensators with minimal McMillan degree and obtained compensators that preserve the L-2 and L-infinity norms of the original system.
- Cristian Oară, Cristian Flutur, Marc Jungers. “Squaring down with zeros cancellation in generalized systems.” Systems Control Letters, 92: 5 – 13, 2016 – Q1 (red zone)
- C. Flutur and C. Oară. “Simultaneous squaring down and zeros cancellation in linear systems.” Control Systems and Computer Science (CSCS), 2015 20th International Conference on, pages 915–922. IEEE, 2015 (first author)
Minimal and Nonminimal Factorizations:
Cristian Flutur addressed the problem of spectral factorization. He introduced the concept of a replicative beam of zeros, enabling the correct deflation of the infinite zero structure, avoiding issues caused by the non-isomorphic correspondence between the rational canonical invariants and the Kronecker canonical structure of the system’s beam. In the non-minimal case, he extended the Wiener-Hopf factorization (previously applicable only to biproper systems) to polynomial, strictly proper, or improper systems.
- C. Flutur, C. Oară. “Minimal factorization for transfer matrices of generalized systems.” System Theory, Control and Computing (ICSTCC), 2014 18th International Conference, pages 13 – 18. IEEE, 2014 (first author)
- C. Oară, C. Flutur. “Nonminimal Spectral Factorization of a Descriptor System.” CEAI, Vol.12, No. 2, pp. 10-16, 2010
Cristian Flutur was a principal researcher in a series of fundamental research projects with national funding, the most relevant being “New Solutions for the Nonlinear Output Regulation Problem” (TE232/2010). In this project, Cristian Flutur investigated the problem of nonlinear state observers and proposed a new normal form, applicable to square non-linear MIMO systems.
- A. Udrea, A. Țiclea, C. Flutur, and V. Tănasă. “On the nonlinear output regulation problem – part 1 – mimo nonlinear systems normal forms and a discussion on the necessary conditions for solving the control problem.” U.P.B. Sci. Bull., Series A, 74(4):3–16, 2012

Înscrierile se fac pe site până pe 8 noiembrie 2012.

Polifest, 3-5 aprilie, Rectorat
30.03.2013
Vă invităm să ne vizitaţi la standul facultăţii de Automatică şi Calculatoare din hol AN (Rectorat) organizat în cadrul Polifest. Sunt prezentate multe din realizările facultăţii. Vor fi jocuri interactive şi roboti.
Programul POLIFEST este disponibil aici.
Elevii interesaţi sunt invitaţi să viziteze laboratoarele facultăţii, atât în zilele Polifest: 3-5 aprilie 2013, cât şi luni şi marţi (1,2 aprilie) în cadrul manifestării “Student pentru o zi“.
La Polifest, vă invităm la prezentările companiilor invitate de facultate, care vor avea loc vineri 5.03 în intervalul 9:00-12:30 în Sala Senatului, Rectorat, astfel:
- 9:00-10:00 – Prezentare Teamnet: Roboţii autonomi, o alternativă în explorarea terestră şi subacvatica
- 10:05-11:30 – Prezentare IBM: Internship la IBM Overview STG Romania Lab IBM, a smarter Global Delivery solution
- 11:30-12:30 – Prezentare Oracle: Big data: Noua paradigmă pentru inovare, competiţie şi productivitate
Vă invităm de asemenea la prezentarea Siveco de joi, 5.03, în intervalul 15-16:30 (sala AN015).

Assistant Professor at UNSTPB, Automatic Control and Systems Eng. dept. (ACSE), with PhD in automation (Univ. Paris Saclay, 2021). His main research interests are optimization-based control and related topics with application in motion planning and fault detection and isolation. As well, he specialized in the application of set theoretic methods to robust control issues, constrained optimization problems and the coupling between motion planning and predictive control. He is (co-)author of 10 journals and conference papers.
Power is nothing without control (F. Dorfler, inControl Podcast)
He obtained his PhD in 2021 at University Paris Saclay, defending the thesis with the title: Safe navigation within cluttered environments. During his PhD, he was part of two French research laboratories: L2S(Laboratory of Signals and System), CentraleSupelec-Univ.Paris Sud – Univ. ParisSaclay, Gif-sur-Yvette and LCIS (Laboratoire de Conception et d’Intégration des Systèmes), Grenoble INP-Univ. Grenoble Alpes, Valence. As well, his expertise is proven by the participation in multiple national research grants (in both fundamental research and robotic applications) such as the project TE-2014-4-2713 “SETS2FTC – Set-theoretic approaches for fault tolerant control of complex systems”, “Young Team” research project PN-III-P1-1.1-TE-2019-1614 “REPLAN – Reliable motion planning for nonlinear systems under uncertainties”. As well, he has been working in the field of MIP (mixed-integer programming)-based control for over 10 years and has developed a broad range of methods for modelling the inherent non-convexities in control related applications. Furthermore, his expertise is complemented by a strong record of collaboration with several laboratories in France and Germany.

I am currently a PhD Candidate and Teaching Assistant in the Automatic Control and Systems Engineering Department of the Polytechnic University of Bucharest (UNSTPB).
